channel.title: The New Beverly Cinema
channel.description: Schedule and program for The New Beverly Cinema (Quentin Tarantino's revival theater in Los Angeles).

item.title: Last Tango in Paris
item.description:
Paul, a middle-aged American living in emotional exile, comes to Paris when his estranged wife commits suicide. Chancing to meet young Frenchwoman named Jeanne, Paul enters into a sadomasochistic, carnal relationship with her, indirectly attacking the hypocrisy all around him and his own feelings of guilt through his raw, outrageous sexual behaviour. (Park Circus)
Cast: Director: Bernardo Bertolucci | Starring: Marlon Brando, Maria Schneider, Maria Michi, Giovanna Galletti, Jean-Pierre Léaud, Massimo Girotti | Year: 1972 | Country: Italy/France | Format: 35mm
Durata: 129 minuti

Orari: Mon March 30 - 7:30 pm

item.title: A Woman in Flames / Belle de Jour
item.description:
A housewife yearning for more abandons her arrogant husband and their upper-class life. While exploring her newfound sexual freedom, she soon discovers that sex can be a very profitable commodity. And as her sexuality intensifies, she forms a complicated new romantic relationship. Catherine Deneuve’s porcelain perfection hides a cracked interior in one of the actress’s most iconic roles: Séverine, a Paris housewife who begins secretly spending her after­noon hours working in a bordello. This surreal and erotic late-sixties daydream from provocateur for the ages Luis Buñuel is an examination of desire and fetishistic pleasure (its characters’ and its viewers’), as well as a gently absurdist take on contemporary social mores and class divisions. Fantasy and reality commingle in this burst of cinematic transgression, which was one of Buñuel’s biggest hits. (Janus Films)
Cast: Director: Robert van Ackeren | Writer: Screenplay by Robert van Ackeren and Catharina Zwerenz | Starring: Gudrun Landgrebe, Mathieu Carriere, Hanns Zischler | Year: 1983 | Country: West Germany | Format: 16mm | Director: Luis Buñuel | Starring: Catherine Deneuve, Jean Sorel, Michel Piccoli | Year: 1967 | Country: France | Format: English subtitled 35mm
Durata: 100 minuti

Orari: Wed March 18 - 7:30 pm / 9:45 pm

item.title: Amuck! / Slaughter Hotel
item.description:
He kidnapped beautiful women, forcing them to do what he wanted… AND HE WANTED EVERYTHING! A secretary hunts for her missing friend but uncovers more than she bargained for in Amuck! , a stylish & sex-crazed Italian giallo from writer-director Silvio Amadio. International beauties Barbara Bouchet & Rosalba Neri star. Cult icons Klaus Kinski, Margaret Lee, and Rosalba Neri in a sex-filled thriller from director Fernando Di Leo! Fernando Di Leo, best known for a series of crime films, tried his hand at horror with this extremely sick little item loaded with nudity and violence. Set at a remote mental institution (inexplicably located in a castle), the film features Klaus Kinski as a doctor whose mostly female patients are being brutally dispatched by a psychopath. Margaret Lee and Rosalba Neri are among the frequently unclothed cast. (Kino Lorber)
Cast: Director: Silvio Amadio | Starring: Farley Granger, Barbara Bouchet, Rosalba Neri, Nino Segurini | Year: 1972 | Director: Fernando Di Leo | Writer: Screenplay & Story by Fernando Di Leo & Nino Latino | Starring: Klaus Kinski, Margaret Lee, Rosalba Neri, Jane Garret | Year: 1971 | Country: Italy | Format: 35mm
Durata: 91 minuti

Orari: Fri March 27 - 6:30 pm / 8:25 pm

item.title: Fellini’s Casanova
item.description:
An extraordinary production four years in the making! The incomparable Federico Fellino directs the visually stunning portrait of Casanova, the infamous Italian womanizer, adventurer, author and libertine. In a remarkable performance, Donald Sutherland portrays the great seducer not as an amorous anomaly, but an everday man living in extraordinary times. Featuring dazzling European settings (although it was filmed entirely in Rome), an unforgettable musical score and Academy Award-winning costumes, Fellini’s Casanova is a cinematic experience to fall in love with. (Universal)
Cast: Director: Federico Fellini | Writer: Story and Screenplay by Federico Fellini and Bernardino Zapponi | Starring: Donald Sutherland, Tina Aumont, Cicely Browne, Carmen Scarpitta, Clara Algranti, Daniela Gatti | Year: 1976 | Country: Italy | Format: 35mm
Durata: 155 minuti

Orari: Fri March 20 - 7:00 pm

item.title: Nights in Black Leather (Adults Only)
item.description:
Eternal self-made gay sex symbol Peter Berlin makes his screen debut as a German immigrant who gets into all sorts of adventures on a trip to San Francisco in this classic from one-and-done filmmaker Ignatio Rutkowski. The closest a gay adult film ever got to the style of Paul Morrissey and Andy Warhol, Nights in Black Leather is both a slyly comedic look at San Francisco’s gay scene and a tribute to the singular commanding presence of Peter Berlin. (Muscle Distribution)
Cast: Director: Richard Abel (as Ignatio Rotkowski) | Starring: Peter Berlin | Year: 1973 | Country: USA | Format: 16mm
Durata: 99 minuti

Orari: Sat March 21 - 11:59 pm

item.title: The Lickerish Quartet / Therese and Isabelle
item.description:
An aristocratic married couple and their adult son decide to end their boring night watching stag films by visiting a traveling carnival. When the trio realize that one of the stars of the stunt show bears a striking resemblance to one of the performers in the film they’ve just watched, they invite her back to their castle for a night of mind-bending fun and games that’ll see each of the members of the family fulfill their deepest fantasies.  Dubbed “an outrageously kinky masterpiece” by Andy Warhol, Radley Metzger’s The Lickerish Quartet is a deliriously psychedelic – yet classily elegant! – classic of softcore erotica. (Muscle Distribution) One of erotic arthouse auteur Radley Metzger’s best loved but rarely screened films follows a pair of young students at a prestigious Swiss boarding school for girls, whose affectionate friendship flowers into something more.  Stunningly shot in black and white Panavision by director of photography Hans Jura and featuring a lush score by Georges Auric. Print courtesy of the Nicolas Winding Refn Collection
Cast: Director: Radley Metzger | Starring: Silvana Venturelli, Frank Wolff, Erika Remberg, Paolo Turco | Year: 1970 | Country: Italy/West Germany/USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Radley Metzger | Writer: Screenplay by Jesse Vogel based on the novel by Violette Leduc | Starring: Essy Persson, Anna Gaël, Barbara Laage, Anne Vernon | Year: 1968 | Country: France/Netherlands/USA/West Germany | Format: 35mm
Durata: 118 minuti

Orari: Sun March 22 - 6:30 pm / 8:30 pm

item.title: The Depraved / Campus Swingers
item.description:
Lena (Christina Lindberg) is a young teen in need of excitement and sexual satisfaction. Torn between her meek and innocent boyfriend Jan and the sadistic party-goer Helge, Lena’s life turns upside-down when Helge takes some nude photographs of her. These photos can destroy her life and Lena must do anything to get them, even if that means having a sadomasochistic encounter with Helge to make him happy. Can she get the photos and hide all this from her family and friends, or will her exploits leave her exposed? (Synapse Films) The fourth film in the long-running German Schoolgirl Report series is, perhaps, its most famous entry, likely due to the inclusion of Swedish sexpot Christina Lindberg. A faux documentary about the sordid lives of teenagers presented across a number of sordid sequences, Campus Swings is a prime slice of taboo-pushing Euro sexploitation.
Cast: Director: Gustav Wiklund | Starring: Christina Lindberg, Heinz Hopf | Year: 1971 | Country: Sweden | Format: 35mm | Director: Ernst Hofbauer | Starring: Christina Lindberg, Ingrid Steeger | Year: 1972 | Country: West Germany | Format: 35mm
Durata: 88 minuti

Orari: Mon March 23 - 7:30 pm / 9:15 pm

item.title: True Romance
item.description:
A rock ‘n roll adventure following two unlikely lovers who accidentally double cross the mob and hit the road towards Los Angeles. Featuring an all-star cast, slick direction from Tony Scott and an endlessly-quotable script by Quentin Tarantino, True Romance will have you saying “you’re so cool” all month long. “This is the kind of movie that creates its own universe, and glories in it. Made with such energy, such high spirits, such an enchanting goofiness, that it’s impossible to resist.” – Roger Ebert “It’s Tarantino’s gutter poetry that detonates True Romance . This movie is dynamite.” – Peter Travers, Rolling Stone
Cast: Director: Tony Scott | Writer: Quentin Tarantino | Starring: Christian Slater, Patricia Arquette, Dennis Hopper, Val Kilmer, Gary Oldman, Brad Pitt, Christopher Walken, Bronson Pinchot, Samuel L. Jackson, Michael Rapaport, Saul Rubinek, Conchata Ferrell and James Gandolfini | Year: 1993 | Country: USA/France | Format: 35mm
Durata: 119 minuti

Orari: Fri March 20 - 11:59 pm

item.title: A Woman in Flames / Belle de Jour
item.description:
A housewife yearning for more abandons her arrogant husband and their upper-class life. While exploring her newfound sexual freedom, she soon discovers that sex can be a very profitable commodity. And as her sexuality intensifies, she forms a complicated new romantic relationship. Catherine Deneuve’s porcelain perfection hides a cracked interior in one of the actress’s most iconic roles: Séverine, a Paris housewife who begins secretly spending her after­noon hours working in a bordello. This surreal and erotic late-sixties daydream from provocateur for the ages Luis Buñuel is an examination of desire and fetishistic pleasure (its characters’ and its viewers’), as well as a gently absurdist take on contemporary social mores and class divisions. Fantasy and reality commingle in this burst of cinematic transgression, which was one of Buñuel’s biggest hits. (Janus Films)
Cast: Director: Robert van Ackeren | Writer: Screenplay by Robert van Ackeren and Catharina Zwerenz | Starring: Gudrun Landgrebe, Mathieu Carriere, Hanns Zischler | Year: 1983 | Country: West Germany | Format: 16mm | Director: Luis Buñuel | Starring: Catherine Deneuve, Jean Sorel, Michel Piccoli | Year: 1967 | Country: France | Format: English subtitled 35mm
Durata: 100 minuti

Orari: Tue March 17 - 7:30 pm / 9:45 pm

item.title: Last Tango in Paris
item.description:
Paul, a middle-aged American living in emotional exile, comes to Paris when his estranged wife commits suicide. Chancing to meet young Frenchwoman named Jeanne, Paul enters into a sadomasochistic, carnal relationship with her, indirectly attacking the hypocrisy all around him and his own feelings of guilt through his raw, outrageous sexual behaviour. (Park Circus)
Cast: Director: Bernardo Bertolucci | Starring: Marlon Brando, Maria Schneider, Maria Michi, Giovanna Galletti, Jean-Pierre Léaud, Massimo Girotti | Year: 1972 | Country: Italy/France | Format: 35mm
Durata: 129 minuti

Orari: Tue March 31 - 7:30 pm

item.title: Fellini’s Casanova
item.description:
An extraordinary production four years in the making! The incomparable Federico Fellino directs the visually stunning portrait of Casanova, the infamous Italian womanizer, adventurer, author and libertine. In a remarkable performance, Donald Sutherland portrays the great seducer not as an amorous anomaly, but an everday man living in extraordinary times. Featuring dazzling European settings (although it was filmed entirely in Rome), an unforgettable musical score and Academy Award-winning costumes, Fellini’s Casanova is a cinematic experience to fall in love with. (Universal)
Cast: Director: Federico Fellini | Writer: Story and Screenplay by Federico Fellini and Bernardino Zapponi | Starring: Donald Sutherland, Tina Aumont, Cicely Browne, Carmen Scarpitta, Clara Algranti, Daniela Gatti | Year: 1976 | Country: Italy | Format: 35mm
Durata: 155 minuti

Orari: Thu March 19 - 7:30 pm

item.title: The Lickerish Quartet / Therese and Isabelle
item.description:
An aristocratic married couple and their adult son decide to end their boring night watching stag films by visiting a traveling carnival. When the trio realize that one of the stars of the stunt show bears a striking resemblance to one of the performers in the film they’ve just watched, they invite her back to their castle for a night of mind-bending fun and games that’ll see each of the members of the family fulfill their deepest fantasies.  Dubbed “an outrageously kinky masterpiece” by Andy Warhol, Radley Metzger’s The Lickerish Quartet is a deliriously psychedelic – yet classily elegant! – classic of softcore erotica. (Muscle Distribution) One of erotic arthouse auteur Radley Metzger’s best loved but rarely screened films follows a pair of young students at a prestigious Swiss boarding school for girls, whose affectionate friendship flowers into something more.  Stunningly shot in black and white Panavision by director of photography Hans Jura and featuring a lush score by Georges Auric. Print courtesy of the Nicolas Winding Refn Collection
Cast: Director: Radley Metzger | Starring: Silvana Venturelli, Frank Wolff, Erika Remberg, Paolo Turco | Year: 1970 | Country: Italy/West Germany/USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Radley Metzger | Writer: Screenplay by Jesse Vogel based on the novel by Violette Leduc | Starring: Essy Persson, Anna Gaël, Barbara Laage, Anne Vernon | Year: 1968 | Country: France/Netherlands/USA/West Germany | Format: 35mm
Durata: 118 minuti

Orari: Sat March 21 - 6:00 pm / 8:00 pm

item.title: Henry & June
item.description:
A searing erotic drama – the first film rated NC-17 by the MPAA – from The Unbearable Lightness of Being director Philip Kaufman. Philip Kaufman’s brilliant film explores the erotic life of two individuals who became 20th Century literary giants. Upon meeting American author Henry Miller (Fred Ward) in Paris, 1931, a young writer named Anaïs Nin (Maria de Medeiros) embarks on a voyage of self-discovery, and faithfully records every experience in her diary. In their search for new truths, Anais and Henry are tantalized by Henry’s hauntingly sensual wife, June (Uma Thurman). Henry & June is an unforgettable journey into the uncharted territory of human relationships, based on the suppressed sections of Anaïs Nin’s diaries. (Universal)
Cast: Director: Philip Kaufman | Writer: screenplay by Philip Kaufman & Rose Kaufman based on the book by Anaïs Nin | Starring: Fred Ward, Uma Thurman, Maria de Medeiros, Richard E. Grant, Kevin Spacey | Year: 1990 | Country: USA/France | Format: 35mm
Durata: 136 minuti

Orari: Tue March 24 - 7:30 pm

item.title: Henry & June
item.description:
A searing erotic drama – the first film rated NC-17 by the MPAA – from The Unbearable Lightness of Being director Philip Kaufman. Philip Kaufman’s brilliant film explores the erotic life of two individuals who became 20th Century literary giants. Upon meeting American author Henry Miller (Fred Ward) in Paris, 1931, a young writer named Anaïs Nin (Maria de Medeiros) embarks on a voyage of self-discovery, and faithfully records every experience in her diary. In their search for new truths, Anais and Henry are tantalized by Henry’s hauntingly sensual wife, June (Uma Thurman). Henry & June is an unforgettable journey into the uncharted territory of human relationships, based on the suppressed sections of Anaïs Nin’s diaries. (Universal)
Cast: Director: Philip Kaufman | Writer: screenplay by Philip Kaufman & Rose Kaufman based on the book by Anaïs Nin | Starring: Fred Ward, Uma Thurman, Maria de Medeiros, Richard E. Grant, Kevin Spacey | Year: 1990 | Country: USA/France | Format: 35mm
Durata: 136 minuti

Orari: Wed March 25 - 7:30 pm

item.title: Amuck! / Slaughter Hotel
item.description:
He kidnapped beautiful women, forcing them to do what he wanted… AND HE WANTED EVERYTHING! A secretary hunts for her missing friend but uncovers more than she bargained for in Amuck! , a stylish & sex-crazed Italian giallo from writer-director Silvio Amadio. International beauties Barbara Bouchet & Rosalba Neri star. Cult icons Klaus Kinski, Margaret Lee, and Rosalba Neri in a sex-filled thriller from director Fernando Di Leo! Fernando Di Leo, best known for a series of crime films, tried his hand at horror with this extremely sick little item loaded with nudity and violence. Set at a remote mental institution (inexplicably located in a castle), the film features Klaus Kinski as a doctor whose mostly female patients are being brutally dispatched by a psychopath. Margaret Lee and Rosalba Neri are among the frequently unclothed cast. (Kino Lorber)
Cast: Director: Silvio Amadio | Starring: Farley Granger, Barbara Bouchet, Rosalba Neri, Nino Segurini | Year: 1972 | Director: Fernando Di Leo | Writer: Screenplay & Story by Fernando Di Leo & Nino Latino | Starring: Klaus Kinski, Margaret Lee, Rosalba Neri, Jane Garret | Year: 1971 | Country: Italy | Format: 35mm
Durata: 91 minuti

Orari: Thu March 26 - 7:30 pm / 9:25 pm

item.title: The Hot Box / Terminal Island
item.description:
Award-winning filmmaker Jonathan Demme ( The Silence of the Lambs ) produces and co-writes The Hot Box , an action-thriller straight from the grimiest corner of the grindhouse. Four nurses working in the tropical Republic of San Rosario enter a hellish nightmare when they are kidnapped and delivered to a rebel army fighting a guerilla war against oppressive government forces. Tormented by every man that crosses their path, the four women must band together in the dense jungle in pursuit of a solitary goal: survival. (Shout! Factory) A cult movie slayer from acclaimed filmmaker Stephanie Rothman ( The Velvet Vampire ), Terminal Island blends violent action with commentary on the American justice system. Carmen Simms has just been sentenced to life imprisonment on Terminal Island – an island created to house vicious criminals in the wake of prison overcrowding. With no guards, no cells, and no laws, Terminal Island has morphed into a defacto society with the tyrannical Bobby ruling over all. As tension and violence mount, Carmen finds her place as the unlikely leader of the island’s underground resistance, which is planning to overthrow Bobby’s reign of terror. Starring Phyllis Davis ( Sweet Sugar ) and Emmy winner Tom Selleck (Magnum P.I.) in one of his earliest roles. (American Genre Film Archive)
Cast: Director: Joe Viola | Writer: Joe Viola and Jonathan Demme | Starring: Andrea Cagan, Margaret Markov, Rickey Richardson, Laurie Rose, Carmen Argenziano, Charles Dierkop | Year: 1972 | Country: Philippines/USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Stephanie Rothman | Writer: Written by Jim Barnett and Charles S. Swartz & Stephanie Rothman | Starring: Phyllis Davis, Don Marshall, Ena Hartman, Marta Kristen, Barbara Leigh, Tom Selleck, Roger E. Mosley | Year: 1973 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 88 minuti

Orari: Sat March 28 - 6:30 pm / 8:30 pm

item.title: Behind the Green Door (Adults Only)
item.description:
As part of a strange club’s elaborate sexual performance, anonymous lovers emerge through a mysterious green door to fulfill a woman’s deepest longings and hidden desires. A low budget, but highly ambitious effort which would become one of the biggest X-rated hits of the 1970s, the Mitchell Brothers’ landmark Behind the Green Door made an international sensation and bonafide star out of its leading lady Marilyn Chambers, while also becoming one of the first hardcore features to win mainstream acclaim for both its technical innovation and artistic merits.
Cast: Director: Jim and Artie Mitchell | Starring: Marilyn Chambers, Johnny Keyes, George McDonald, Ben Davidson | Year: 1972 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 72 minuti

Orari: Sat March 28 - 11:59 pm

item.title: Forbidden Letters (Adults Only) / The Night Before (1973)(Adults Only)
item.description:
Shot in the midst of the gay community’s fight against Anita Bryant’s “Save Our Children” campaign, this critically acclaimed feature from Arthur J. Bressan, Jr. ( Gay USA , Buddies ) is a touching look at memory, fantasy, and the psychological tolls of the closet. Larry, a young gay man, is trying to pass the time on the day his older lover Richard is set to be released from prison. Unable to clear his head through casual sex, he reads through his letters to Richard – letters he knew he could never send out of fear that an outing would lead to a harsher sentence. As Richard’s release draws nearer, will the spark still be there when he gets out? (American Genre Film Archive) Pioneering pornographer and playwright Arch Brown landmark classic follows a handsome New York slacker with no real ambition in life other than to meet all the interesting people in the city in a series of surreal sexual adventures.
Cast: Director: Arthur J. Bressan, Jr | Writer: Arthur J. Bressan, Jr | Starring: Richard Locke, Robert Adams, Victoria Young | Year: 1979 | Country: USA | Format: 16mm | Director: Arch Brown | Writer: Arch Brown and Bruce Brown | Starring: Coke Hennessy, Michael Kade, Bill Yort, Mimmi Garth | Year: 1973 | Country: USA | Format: 16mm
Durata: 72 minuti

Orari: Sun March 15 - 6:30 pm / 8:15 pm

item.title: The Hot Box / Terminal Island
item.description:
Award-winning filmmaker Jonathan Demme ( The Silence of the Lambs ) produces and co-writes The Hot Box , an action-thriller straight from the grimiest corner of the grindhouse. Four nurses working in the tropical Republic of San Rosario enter a hellish nightmare when they are kidnapped and delivered to a rebel army fighting a guerilla war against oppressive government forces. Tormented by every man that crosses their path, the four women must band together in the dense jungle in pursuit of a solitary goal: survival. (Shout! Factory) A cult movie slayer from acclaimed filmmaker Stephanie Rothman ( The Velvet Vampire ), Terminal Island blends violent action with commentary on the American justice system. Carmen Simms has just been sentenced to life imprisonment on Terminal Island – an island created to house vicious criminals in the wake of prison overcrowding. With no guards, no cells, and no laws, Terminal Island has morphed into a defacto society with the tyrannical Bobby ruling over all. As tension and violence mount, Carmen finds her place as the unlikely leader of the island’s underground resistance, which is planning to overthrow Bobby’s reign of terror. Starring Phyllis Davis ( Sweet Sugar ) and Emmy winner Tom Selleck (Magnum P.I.) in one of his earliest roles. (American Genre Film Archive)
Cast: Director: Joe Viola | Writer: Joe Viola and Jonathan Demme | Starring: Andrea Cagan, Margaret Markov, Rickey Richardson, Laurie Rose, Carmen Argenziano, Charles Dierkop | Year: 1972 | Country: Philippines/USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Stephanie Rothman | Writer: Written by Jim Barnett and Charles S. Swartz & Stephanie Rothman | Starring: Phyllis Davis, Don Marshall, Ena Hartman, Marta Kristen, Barbara Leigh, Tom Selleck, Roger E. Mosley | Year: 1973 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 88 minuti

Orari: Sun March 29 - 6:30 pm / 8:30 pm

item.title: True Romance
item.description:
A rock ‘n roll adventure following two unlikely lovers who accidentally double cross the mob and hit the road towards Los Angeles. Featuring an all-star cast, slick direction from Tony Scott and an endlessly-quotable script by Quentin Tarantino, True Romance will have you saying “you’re so cool” all month long. “This is the kind of movie that creates its own universe, and glories in it. Made with such energy, such high spirits, such an enchanting goofiness, that it’s impossible to resist.” – Roger Ebert “It’s Tarantino’s gutter poetry that detonates True Romance . This movie is dynamite.” – Peter Travers, Rolling Stone
Cast: Director: Tony Scott | Writer: Quentin Tarantino | Starring: Christian Slater, Patricia Arquette, Dennis Hopper, Val Kilmer, Gary Oldman, Brad Pitt, Christopher Walken, Bronson Pinchot, Samuel L. Jackson, Michael Rapaport, Saul Rubinek, Conchata Ferrell and James Gandolfini | Year: 1993 | Country: USA/France | Format: 35mm
Durata: 119 minuti

Orari: Fri March 27 - 11:59 pm

item.title: Venus in Furs / Succubus
item.description:
Of all the twisted hits from cult director Jess Franco, this is the one that fans and critics alike call his masterpiece! James Darren stars as a traumatized trumpeter sucked into a world of psycho-sexual horror along with his sultry girlfriend (singer Barbara McNair), a kinky lesbian (Margaret Lee of The Bloody Judge ) and the mysterious, insatiable beauty (luscious Maria Rohm of Justine ) who may lead them all straight to hell. Dennis Price ( Vampyros Lesbos ) co-stars in this infamous erotic shocker that also features an outstanding jazz score by British rockers Manfred Mann. (Blue Underground) At a chic nightclub for jaded jetsetters, a simulated snuff performance is the main attraction, and Lorna, its leading lady, is the obsession of men and women alike. Little do they realize that Lorna’s Mephistophelian manager has transformed her into “the essence of evil… a devil on earth!” Soon, the sensuous starlet’s stage persona possesses her entirely, thrusting Lorna into a hallucinatory world of sadomasochistic excess and drawing everyone she touches into her debaucherous delirium. Can anyone stop Lorna before her perverse stage show becomes a murderous reality? Starring the beautiful Janine Reynaud, Jack Taylor and Howard Vernon, Jess Franco’s Faustian fever dream provides the blue-print for the erotic horror that has become the infamous director’s enduring legacy. (Blue Underground)
Cast: Director: Jess Franco | Writer: Screenplay by Jess Franco and Malvin Wald | Starring: James Darren, Barbara McNair, Maria Rohm, Klaus Kinski, Dennis Price, Margaret Lee, Aldo Lastretti | Year: 1969 | Country: UK/Italy/West Germany | Format: 35mm | Director: Jess Franco | Writer: Screenplay by Pier A. Caminnecci | Starring: Janine Reynaud, Jack Taylor, Adrian Hoven, Howard Vernon | Year: 1968 | Country: West Germany | Format: 35mm
Durata: 80 minuti

Orari: Mon March 16 - 7:30 pm / 9:25 pm
